user_message: Working on Woodgrove Labs's pipeline hygiene. Got the escalation email -- help me run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-01-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:10:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:17:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:22:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:29:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:34:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:36:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-A trap (Architecture summary states customer-environment facts that were assumed not con); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-A failure mode (Architecture summary states customer-environment facts that were assumed not con)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-A -- Architecture summary states customer-environment facts that were assumed not con
